title: scan db v0
description: Implement crucible scan db: inspect a legacy database or exported metadata and emit catalog records plus derived claims. Extraction targets: tables, columns, constraints (PK, FK, NOT NULL, CHECK, UNIQUE), views, stored procedures, accessible job metadata. Catalog channel: table/column/constraint/view/procedure metadata records with provenance. Claim channel: claim.v0 only for ambiguous usage/liveness propositions. Inventory channel: inventory record for every scanned object. Day-one adapter: SQL Server or Postgres metadata (at least one). Definition of done: (1) can scan a test database and emit catalog records for all extraction targets, (2) claims emitted only for genuinely ambiguous propositions (not mechanically observable facts), (3) every emitted record has a provenance locator, (4) inventory covers all scanned objects, (5) scan report section summarizing covered objects and blind spots, (6) fixture test: scan a known DB, assert expected catalog/claim/inventory output.

title: Project bootstrap: stack, structure, build system
description:
Choose Python (aligns with cmdrvl-cli ecosystem). Init project with pyproject.toml, src layout, test runner (pytest). Wire crucible entry point.

Files: pyproject.toml, src/crucible/__init__.py, src/crucible/__main__.py, tests/conftest.py

Definition of done:
- crucible --help prints usage
- pytest runs green (zero tests is OK)
- ruff check + ty check pass
- project installs cleanly via pip install -e .

title: Freeze neighborhood graph schema
description:
Produce spec document: docs/spec/neighborhood_schema.md

Define node types (referencing catalog/claim/inventory records), edge types (dependency, lineage, reference), readiness classification enum and derivation logic, serialization format for nodes.json and edges.json, traversal semantics.

Files: docs/spec/neighborhood_schema.md

Definition of done:
- Node type vocabulary with concrete JSON examples
- Edge type vocabulary with cardinality rules
- Readiness derivation as a decision table: {catalog-only → observed, catalog+claims → contested, inventory-only → inventoried, edge-only → referenced}
- Serialization format frozen with worked example
- Seed traversal semantics defined (BFS outward from target, stop conditions)
- Determinism requirements specified

title: Catalog lineage writer
description:
Implement lineage graph node/edge record emission for observed data flow paths.

Files: src/crucible/catalog/lineage.py, tests/catalog/test_lineage.py

Implements:
- Lineage node builder: graph node records for entities in observed data flow
- Lineage edge builder: directed edges representing observed data movement
- Alignment with cmdrvl-cli metadata lineage graph structures

Definition of done:
- Lineage nodes and edges match cmdrvl-cli metadata lineage graph shapes
- Test: simple lineage path (table → view → report) serializes correctly
- Test: deterministic output for identical lineage inputs
- Provenance attached to every lineage record

title: Inventory writer module
description: Implement deterministic inventory record writer. Each scanned input gets an inventory record with: artifact_id (sha256 of artifact content), source classification (parsed vs inventoried-only), provenance pointer, scanner identity. Definition of done: (1) artifact_id generation is deterministic and content-addressed, (2) parsed-vs-inventoried-only classification explicit in every record, (3) output written to inventory/ directory per the five-part layout, (4) deterministic output — same inputs produce identical inventory files, (5) unit tests for artifact_id stability and classification correctness.

title: Freeze claim.v0 contract
description:
Produce spec document: docs/spec/claim_v0.md

Freeze all enums (source.kind, subject.kind, property_type, locator.kind), field types, value shapes per property_type, subject-ID conventions, normalization rules, and claim_id computation algorithm.

Files: docs/spec/claim_v0.md

Definition of done:
- Every frozen enum value has a concrete example
- Every property_type has a worked value-shape example
- claim_id computation has a byte-level worked example (input JSON → canonical form → sha256 → claim_id)
- Subject-ID normalization rules cover all 10 subject.kind values with examples
- Normalization rules are unambiguous enough to implement without design questions

title: scan files v0
description: Implement crucible scan files: inventory and parse local artifact directories. Recursive file inventory of the target directory. High-value day-one adapters: scheduler exports (cron/autosys), log files (extract timestamps and referenced objects), structured extracts (CSV/JSON with column headers). Catalog channel: metadata records for parseable file/report/export artifacts. Claim channel: claim.v0 where adapters yield only inferential or contested propositions. Inventory-only fallback: files without a matching adapter get inventory records with artifact_id and provenance but no parsed claims. Definition of done: (1) recursive inventory covers all files in target directory, (2) at least two adapters parse real artifact types, (3) unparsed files get inventory-only records (not silently skipped), (4) scan report section lists parsed vs inventoried-only counts, (5) fixture test with mixed parseable and unparseable files.

title: Determinism test suite
description: Comprehensive test suite proving scan outputs are deterministic and correctly classified. Tests: (1) Identical input rerun: run same scan twice, assert catalog, claims, inventory, neighborhoods, and report are byte-identical. (2) Mixed-source fixture: one bounded legacy slice scanned across db + repo + files, assert full output correctness. (3) Normalization: subject-id normalization for every kind, claim_id hashing stability across serialization round-trips. (4) Channel classification: fixture proving observed facts stay on catalog channel and only inferred/ambiguous facts become claims. (5) Neighborhood stability: same inputs produce identical graph structure and readiness classifications. Definition of done: all five test categories implemented, passing, and running in CI.

title: Canonical serializer and claim_id builder
description:
Implement deterministic JSON serialization and content-addressed claim_id computation.

Files: src/crucible/claim/serializer.py, tests/claim/test_serializer.py

Implements:
- canonical_json(claim) → sorted keys, no whitespace variation, deterministic byte output
- claim_id(claim) → sha256 of canonical JSON of all fields except claim_id
- Normalization: arrays sorted where order is not semantic, strings trimmed, repeated whitespace collapsed for free-text fields

Definition of done:
- canonical_json produces byte-identical output for semantically equivalent inputs
- claim_id round-trip: build claim → compute claim_id → re-serialize → re-compute → assert identical
- Test: two claims differing only in key order produce identical canonical JSON
- Test: claim_id is stable across Python versions (no dict ordering dependency)
- Test: normalization rules applied (sorted arrays, trimmed strings)

title: scan db: liveness claims
description:
Implement claim-channel logic for ambiguous usage and liveness assessments in scan db.

Files: src/crucible/scan/db/liveness.py, tests/scan/db/test_liveness.py

Implements:
- Liveness assessment: query table statistics, row counts, last-modified timestamps where available
- Claim emission: liveness claims (alive/stale/unknown/dead) with confidence scores
- Only emit claims for genuinely ambiguous propositions — if a table has recent rows, that's catalog-level (observed), not a claim

Definition of done:
- Liveness claims emitted only for ambiguous cases (not for clearly active or clearly empty tables)
- Confidence scores reflect evidence strength
- Claims use correct value shape (liveness_value)
- Fixture test: DB with active, stale, and empty tables → correct claims + correct non-emission for unambiguous cases
- Channel classification test: observed liveness facts stay catalog-side, only ambiguous assessments become claims

title: scan repo v0
description: Implement crucible scan repo: inspect codebases that touch the legacy system. Extraction targets: SQL string literals (Java, Python), connection/config discovery, ORM/model metadata where obvious, report definition files where parseable, test/enum/constant files for valid-value and semantic-label hints. Catalog channel: metadata records for directly observed applications, reports, feeds, consumers, and unambiguous dependencies. Claim channel: claim.v0 for inferred reads, writes, valid values, semantic labels, and consumer dependencies requiring interpretation. Every claim carries file-range provenance (file path + line range). Definition of done: (1) can scan a test repo with embedded SQL and emit correct catalog + claim records, (2) SQL extraction works for Java and Python string literals, (3) config/connection discovery finds common patterns (JDBC URLs, SQLAlchemy strings, .env references), (4) file-range provenance on every output, (5) fixture test with known repo structure.

title: Metadata API conformance tests
description:
Validate that all catalog output shapes conform to cmdrvl-cli metadata input types. Safety net against breaking the existing metadata pipeline.

Files: tests/conformance/test_metadata_shapes.py, tests/conformance/test_link_shapes.py

Implements:
- Table record shape validation against TableInput schema
- File record shape validation against FileInput schema
- Resource record shape validation against ResourceInput schema
- Link record shape validation against LinkInput schema
- Lineage record shape validation against lineage graph schema
- Import or snapshot cmdrvl-cli metadata type definitions as test fixtures

Definition of done:
- Every catalog record type validated against its metadata API counterpart
- Tests fail if catalog output drifts from metadata API shapes
- At least one negative test per record type (malformed record detected)
- Tests run in CI as a regression gate

title: CLI shell: crucible scan subcommands
description:
Wire the crucible CLI with scan command family and shared output infrastructure.

Files: src/crucible/cli.py, src/crucible/scan/__init__.py, tests/test_cli.py

Implements:
- crucible scan db, crucible scan repo, crucible scan files subcommands
- Shared output directory creation: catalog/, claims/, inventory/, neighborhoods/, scan-report.json
- Common option parsing: --output-dir, --verbose
- Stub implementations that create the five-part directory structure

Definition of done:
- crucible scan db --help, crucible scan repo --help, crucible scan files --help all print usage
- Any scan invocation creates the five-part output directory structure
- Each subcommand accepts minimum required input (connection string / repo path / directory path)
- Test: CLI dispatch reaches correct subcommand handler
- Test: output directory structure is correct

title: Freeze catalog target model
description:
Produce spec document: docs/spec/catalog_model.md

Read cmdrvl-cli metadata shapes (Table/TableInput, FileResponse/FileInput, ResourceInput, LinkInput, lineage graph). Document the mapping from crucible scan observations to metadata API types. Decide write-path strategy.

Files: docs/spec/catalog_model.md

Definition of done:
- Each scan entity type mapped to its metadata API equivalent with concrete JSON examples
- Write-path strategy decided: direct API, staged JSON, or local snapshot
- Provenance attachment convention defined
- Resource type vocabulary for apps, jobs, reports, feeds, mappings, consumers defined

title: Catalog base writer and table records
description:
Implement the deterministic catalog record writer base and Table/TableInput-shaped record emission.

Files: src/crucible/catalog/writer.py, src/crucible/catalog/tables.py, tests/catalog/test_tables.py

Implements:
- CatalogWriter base: deterministic JSON output, provenance attachment, write to catalog/ directory
- Table record builder: Table/TableInput-shaped records for DB objects (tables and columns)
- Provenance convention: each record carries source scanner identity and locator

Definition of done:
- Table records match cmdrvl-cli metadata TableInput shape
- Output is deterministic: same inputs → byte-identical catalog/tables.json
- Provenance attached to every record
- Test: round-trip table record serialization
- Test: determinism — two runs produce identical output

title: scan files: scheduler export adapter
description:
Implement adapter for parsing scheduler export files (cron, Autosys, Control-M).

Files: src/crucible/scan/files/scheduler_adapter.py, tests/scan/files/test_scheduler.py

Implements:
- Cron export parsing: extract job names, schedules, dependencies
- Autosys/Control-M export parsing: extract job definitions, conditions, calendars
- Catalog channel: resource records for discovered jobs, link records for job dependencies
- Claim channel: schedule claims with value shape {"kind":"cron|event|manual|unknown","value":"...","timezone":"..."}

Definition of done:
- Parses at least one scheduler export format (cron at minimum)
- Job records emit as catalog resources
- Schedule information emits as claims with correct value shape
- Provenance attached to every output
- Fixture test: known scheduler export → expected catalog + claim output

title: Value helpers and shape validation
description:
Implement value constructors and validators for each property_type value shape.

Files: src/crucible/claim/values.py, tests/claim/test_values.py

Implements:
- exists_value() → true
- schema_value(obj) → validated normalized object
- constraint_value(kind, detail) → {"kind":"not_null|pk|fk|check|unique","detail":{...}}
- subject_ref(kind, id) → {"kind":"...","id":"..."} (reusable for reads, writes, depends_on, used_by, authoritative_for)
- schedule_value(kind, value, timezone) → validated schedule object
- valid_values_value(values) → sorted, de-duplicated array of strings
- semantic_label_value(label) → normalized string
- liveness_value(status) → one of alive|stale|unknown|dead
- validate_value(property_type, value) → raises on shape mismatch

Definition of done:
- One constructor per property_type
- validate_value rejects malformed values with descriptive error for each property_type
- Test: valid_values auto-sorts and de-duplicates: ["b","a","a"] → ["a","b"]
- Test: liveness rejects invalid status strings
- Test: subject_ref rejects invalid subject.kind values

title: scan files: scaffold + recursive inventory
description:
Implement the scan files command core: recursive directory inventory with fallback.

Files: src/crucible/scan/files/__init__.py, src/crucible/scan/files/inventory.py, tests/scan/files/test_inventory.py

Implements:
- scan files main loop: recursively walk target directory
- Catalog channel: file metadata records for every discovered file
- Inventory channel: inventory record for every file with artifact_id + parsed/inventoried_only classification
- Inventory-only fallback: files without a matching adapter get inventoried but not parsed (never silently skipped)
- Adapter dispatch hook: extensible dispatch to file-type-specific adapters

Definition of done:
- Recursive inventory covers all files including nested directories
- Every file gets an inventory record (no silent skipping)
- Unparsed files classified as inventoried_only
- Catalog file records match cmdrvl-cli metadata FileInput shapes
- Fixture test: directory with mixed file types → correct inventory
- Determinism: two runs against same directory produce identical output

title: scan repo: enum/constant scanning
description:
Implement enum and constant scanning for valid_values and semantic_label extraction.

Files: src/crucible/scan/repo/enum_scanner.py, tests/scan/repo/test_enums.py

Implements:
- Python enum extraction: Enum subclasses, string constant lists, dict lookups
- Java enum extraction: enum declarations, static final String arrays
- Test file scanning: assert statements and test data that reveal valid value sets
- Claim channel: valid_values claims (sorted array of strings), semantic_label claims

Definition of done:
- Extracts Python Enum members and constant lists
- Extracts Java enum values
- Claims use correct value shapes (valid_values → sorted array, semantic_label → normalized string)
- File-range provenance on every claim
- Fixture test: repo with known enums/constants → expected valid_values and semantic_label claims

title: Catalog writer module
description: Implement deterministic catalog record writer aligned with cmdrvl-cli metadata model. Support emitting: table metadata records, file metadata records, custom resource records (for apps, jobs, reports, feeds, mappings, consumers), link records (for direct dependencies), lineage graph node/edge records. Provenance attachment for every observed fact. Definition of done: (1) can emit all five catalog record types with correct shapes, (2) output is deterministic — same inputs produce byte-identical catalog files, (3) provenance attached to every record, (4) writes to catalog/ output directory in the five-part layout, (5) unit tests for each record type with round-trip serialization.

title: scan db: scaffold + table/column/constraint extraction
description:
Implement the scan db command core: Postgres metadata adapter for tables, columns, and constraints.

Files: src/crucible/scan/db/__init__.py, src/crucible/scan/db/postgres.py, tests/scan/db/test_tables.py

Implements:
- scan db main loop: connect to DB (or read exported metadata), iterate schemas/tables
- Postgres metadata adapter: query information_schema / pg_catalog for tables, columns, constraints (PK, FK, NOT NULL, CHECK, UNIQUE)
- Catalog channel: table/column metadata records with provenance locators
- Inventory channel: inventory record for every scanned schema/table
- Optional include/exclude filters for schemas and tables

Definition of done:
- Can scan a test Postgres DB and emit correct catalog table records
- Constraints (PK, FK, NOT NULL, CHECK, UNIQUE) extracted and attached to table records
- Every emitted record has a db_object provenance locator
- Inventory record for every scanned object
- Catalog output validates against cmdrvl-cli metadata TableInput shapes
- Fixture test: known DB schema → expected catalog + inventory output
- Determinism: two runs against same DB produce identical output

title: scan repo: scaffold + SQL extraction
description:
Implement the scan repo command core: file walker + SQL string literal extraction for Python and Java.

Files: src/crucible/scan/repo/__init__.py, src/crucible/scan/repo/sql_extractor.py, tests/scan/repo/test_sql.py

Implements:
- scan repo main loop: walk repo files, dispatch to extractors by file type
- SQL string literal extraction: find SQL strings in .py and .java files
- Parse extracted SQL to identify table/column references
- Claim channel: reads/writes claims for inferred table access with file-range provenance
- Inventory channel: inventory record for every scanned file

Definition of done:
- Can scan a test repo and extract SQL literals from Python and Java files
- Python patterns: f-strings, triple-quoted strings, SQLAlchemy text(), cursor.execute()
- Java patterns: String literals, PreparedStatement, JDBC patterns
- Every claim has file-range provenance (file path + line range)
- Fixture test: known repo with embedded SQL → expected claims
- Determinism: two runs against same repo produce identical output

title: Integration determinism test suite
description:
Cross-module integration tests proving end-to-end determinism and correct channel classification.

Files: tests/integration/test_determinism.py, tests/integration/test_channel_boundary.py

Implements:
- Full pipeline rerun test: same inputs → byte-identical catalog, claims, inventory, neighborhoods, and report
- Mixed-source fixture: one bounded legacy slice scanned across db + repo + files, assert full output correctness
- Channel classification boundary test: fixture proving observed facts → catalog channel, inferred/ambiguous facts → claim channel
- Neighborhood stability: same inputs → identical graph structure and readiness classifications

Definition of done:
- Pipeline rerun test passes (byte-identical output across two runs)
- Channel boundary test with at least 5 observed facts and 5 inferred facts, each landing on correct channel
- Neighborhood graph identical across reruns
- All tests running in CI

title: Project bootstrap: stack, structure, build system
description: Choose language/runtime (Python recommended given cmdrvl-cli ecosystem), init project structure, configure build system and test runner, wire entry point. Definition of done: (1) crucible --help prints usage with scan subcommand group, (2) test suite runs green with zero tests, (3) project installs cleanly from source, (4) CI lint/typecheck/test pipeline defined.

title: Evidence neighborhood builder
description:
Build the evidence neighborhood graph from combined scan outputs with readiness classification.

Files: src/crucible/neighborhood/builder.py, src/crucible/neighborhood/traversal.py, tests/neighborhood/test_builder.py

Implements:
- Node aggregation: each unique subject across catalog, claims, and inventory becomes a graph node
- Edge derivation: dependency links, lineage edges, and cross-surface subject references become graph edges
- Readiness classification per node: observed (catalog only) | contested (catalog+claims) | inventoried (inventory only) | referenced (edge-only, never directly scanned)
- Seed traversal: BFS outward from a target subject to discover full evidence neighborhood
- Deterministic serialization: nodes.json + edges.json, content-addressed

Definition of done:
- Nodes aggregated from all three output channels
- Cross-surface edges correct: scan db subjects linked to scan repo subjects through shared table references
- Readiness classification matches decision table from frozen schema
- Seed traversal returns correct connected subgraph
- Determinism: same inputs → byte-identical graph output
- Fixture test: three-surface scan fixture → expected graph structure, readiness, and traversal result

title: Subject-id normalizer
description:
Implement normalization rules for all subject.kind ID conventions.

Files: src/crucible/claim/normalize.py, tests/claim/test_normalize.py

Implements:
- normalize_subject_id(kind, raw_id) → normalized string
- Rules: DB identifiers lowercased, paths use / separators, schema.table.column dot-separated
- Convention coverage: table (schema.table), column (schema.table.column), view, procedure, job (system.name), report, feed, artifact (normalized path), consumer, mapping

Definition of done:
- One test per subject.kind with raw → normalized assertion
- Case normalization: "FIN.GL_ACTUALS" → "fin.gl_actuals"
- Path normalization: "exports\\hfm\\rules\\file.csv" → "exports/hfm/rules/file.csv"
- Whitespace handling: " fin.gl_actuals " → "fin.gl_actuals"
- Rejects empty or obviously malformed IDs with clear error

title: Freeze claim.v0 contract
description: Finalize frozen enums (source.kind, subject.kind, property_type, locator.kind), field types, value shapes per property_type, subject-ID conventions, and normalization rules as a testable spec artifact. Definition of done: (1) spec document covers all frozen fields from the plan, (2) includes concrete examples for every enum value and property_type value shape, (3) normalization rules are unambiguous enough to implement without reopening design questions, (4) claim_id computation algorithm fully specified with a worked example.

title: Claim types and enums
description:
Implement all frozen enums and the Claim data model from the claim.v0 spec.

Files: src/crucible/claim/types.py, tests/claim/test_types.py

Implements:

- SubjectKind enum (table, column, view, procedure, job, report, feed, artifact, consumer, mapping)
- PropertyType enum (exists, schema, constraint, reads, writes, depends_on, used_by, schedule, valid_values, semantic_label, liveness, authoritative_for)
- LocatorKind enum (db_object, file_range, export_row, log_line, config_key, manual_note)
- Claim dataclass/model with all frozen fields
- SubjectRef dataclass

Definition of done:
- All enums exhaustive (no catch-all/Other variant)
- Claim model enforces frozen field types at construction
- Round-trip: construct → serialize → deserialize → assert equal
- Tests: one test per enum verifying all members present

title: Freeze catalog target model
description: Document alignment between crucible scan catalog outputs and cmdrvl-cli metadata shapes. Map each scan entity to its metadata API equivalent: DB objects to Table/TableInput, files to FileResponse/FileInput, applications/jobs/reports/feeds/mappings/consumers to ResourceInput, dependencies to LinkInput, lineage to graph nodes/edges. title: CLI shell: crucible scan subcommands

Definition of done: (1) crucible scan db --help, crucible scan repo --help, crucible scan files --help all print usage, (2) output directory created with correct structure on any scan invocation, (3) scan subcommands accept their minimum required inputs (connection string / repo path / directory path), (4) stub implementations that create empty output files in the correct layout.

title: Hyperion slice 1 end-to-end test
description: Full end-to-end scan of one bounded Hyperion outcome slice across all surfaces. Choose one real outcome (e.g., close pack report family). Run crucible scan db against the relevant database surface, crucible scan repo against the relevant codebases, crucible scan files against surrounding artifacts. Verify: catalog hydrates cleanly without ad hoc translation, evidence neighborhood is navigable from the target outcome to its full dependency boundary, readiness classification shows a realistic mix of observed/contested/inventoried/referenced, claims are consumable by decoding without translation glue, scan report accurately reflects coverage and gaps. Definition of done: (1) one real outcome slice fully scanned, (2) catalog records land in metadata-aligned shapes, (3) neighborhood graph navigable from target outcome, (4) scan report shows realistic coverage, (5) derived claims validated as consumable by decoding.

title: Scan report generator
description: Generate scan-report.json summarizing all scan outputs. Content: catalog counts by node type (tables, files, resources, links, lineage edges) and edge type, claim counts by source.kind and property_type, inventoried-only counts, neighborhood topology (node count by readiness classification, edge count by type, reachable subgraph size from target outcome), blind-spot summary (subjects referenced but not directly scanned), subject coverage summary. Definition of done: (1) report includes all specified count categories, (2) neighborhood topology section present with correct aggregates, (3) blind-spot summary correctly identifies referenced-but-unscanned subjects, (4) report is valid JSON and deterministic, (5) fixture test: known scan outputs produce expected report.

title: Hyperion slice 1 end-to-end test
description:
Full end-to-end scan of one bounded Hyperion outcome slice across all surfaces.

Files: tests/e2e/test_hyperion_slice.py, tests/e2e/fixtures/ (test data)

Choose one real outcome (e.g., close pack report family). Run crucible scan db + scan repo + scan files against representative test fixtures.

Definition of done:
- One real outcome slice fully scanned across all three surfaces
- Catalog records land in metadata-aligned shapes (conformance tests pass)
- Evidence neighborhood navigable from target outcome to full dependency boundary
- Readiness classification shows realistic mix (observed/contested/inventoried/referenced)
- Scan report accurately reflects coverage and gaps
- Derived claims consumable by decoding without translation glue
- Blind spots explicitly surfaced in scan report

title: Evidence neighborhood builder
description: Build the evidence neighborhood graph from combined scan outputs. Aggregate nodes from catalog records, claims, and inventory entries (each unique subject becomes a node). Derive edges from lineage links, dependency links, and subject references across scan surfaces (scan db nodes connect to scan repo nodes through shared table/view references, etc.). Compute per-node readiness classification: observed (catalog only, no claims), contested (catalog + claims), inventoried (inventory only), referenced (mentioned by edges but never directly scanned). Support traversal from a seed subject outward to discover the full evidence neighborhood. Emit deterministic, content-addressed nodes.json and edges.json. Definition of done: (1) node aggregation covers all three output channels, (2) cross-surface edges correctly link subjects across scan db/repo/files, (3) readiness classification matches the decision table from the frozen schema, (4) seed traversal returns the correct connected subgraph, (5) output is deterministic, (6) fixture test: three-surface scan fixture, assert correct graph structure and readiness.
